summary: Saudi Aramco is negotiating with Ukrainian drone manufacturers SkyFall and Wild Hornets to acquire interceptor drones for protecting oil fields, while also engaging with Phantom Defense for electronic warfare systems. Russian forces are deploying FPV drones in coordinated formations of 3-5 units to counter Ukrainian Mavic drones, with operators reporting successful engagements. The "Yolka" air defense drone has conducted multiple kinetic intercepts of Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs in spring 2026, demonstrating active Russian counter-UAS operations. Meanwhile, Ukrainian drone procurement has doubled year-over-year, with increased deliveries of Mavic, Autel, and Matrice systems, and the Dignitas Foundation is recruiting fixed-wing UAV instructors for military training. On the tactical front, Russian infantry have engaged Ukrainian FPV drones in close combat, including a reported bayonet charge, while Ukrainian forces continue to strike Russian positions with coordinated FPV attacks.
